title: Electron Microscopic Study of Carbon Powders and Their Composites
author: Zhogashtiev N.
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Primary ultrafine carbon powders were obtained using the sol-gel method and drying in a supercritical fluid using various carbon raw materials. By heat treatment at 1000–1100 °C, ultrafine carbon powders were obtained. The properties of primary ultrafine carbon powders and their annealing products were studied using a Tescan Vega 3 SEM scanning electron microscope (SEM). The results show that the starting materials affect the structure of the resulting ultrafine carbon powders.
